Interesting idea.   But unless the users are content to live in a railyard, the cost of building the  "Romanichel Village" infrastructure would be prohibitive.     There's the cost of the land (and expect a lot of NIMBYism against them), the cost of building the rail infrastructure, and the cost of building the service infrastructure (water, electricity, waste, etc).      It would be more feasible, realistic, and affordable to base this concept using existing cargo containers as the footprint for a semi-mobile home. Simply putting fresh fuel in the reactor is cheaper than processing the now-unusable fuel into usable fuel.  Scientifically, it is simpler to change the design. Growing up in Iowa on a large farm we had the Chicago Great Western  railway go thru our property.  I am talking about the 50"s, 60's, 70's.  Hobos rode the empty cars on a daily basis.  When they were near a farm, they would hop off, come up to the farm house and politely ask for any leftover food.  On a farm, there is always plenty of food.  My Mother would give them fresh baked bread and butter, we had our own dairy and butter churn etc., , soup, milk, left over  cooked beef roast, pork, chicken etc.   Back then, the hobos were respectable.  They never stole, or committed any crimes  and usually after receiving food, they would ask if there was any work they could do in appreciation of the food they received.  We would usually have them move some hay bales, feed the chickens, (200), muck out a stall or two.  Then, they would hop the train, and be gone. I am curious about why sea otters cannot be returned to the Wild after they recover. 
True story.
There was a black bear, who as a a small cub lost his mother towards the end of summer. 
People did not to call Fish and Game because the cub would be euthanized.
 Instead they called a bear sanctuary in Canada known for successfully reintroducing bears to the wild. 
They were given information on what to do (stay away from the cub) keep him from all things human, no feeding needed, as he was seen successfully catching fish and eating berries.
Somehow he made it through to Fall, by staying away from other bears and humans. 
The area where he moved around was mostly private land.
Winter approached, the sanctuary was consulted again.  
He never saw the humans who carefully, daily, left a trail of salmon to the den. 
Everyone figured he would die that winter.
 But the following Spring, and for the last eight seasons, the cub with the distinctive white chest has returned as a grown man-bear.

First of all, you're right, we would certainly prefer if HART or the City were developing dedicated renewable electricity production to power the rail system. But ultimately, as you may be aware, we fought for many years to get the 100% RPS standard by 2045 passed two years ago.  No matter where the rail system gets it power, whether purchased from HECO, or HART owned power generating facilities; we are 100% confident it will be 100% renewable by 2045 at the latest.

What's interesting is that a 1986 study found that a flubendazole-mebendazole 7-to-10-day treatment killed in the range of 93-99% of rat lungworm larvae (this study was in rats).  So we have longstanding study data to back up experience in human use. Neither drug has common side effects beyond gastric distress in p.o. usage, and serious side effects are uncommon.  Obviously the prescribing MD would need to assess the patient for allergy potential, but in general the contraindications would be few.

We also need to consider the best "rough and ready" diagnostic tool.  In fact that's almost trivial.  A small but significant number (or percentage) of eosinophils in the CSF is diagnostic of eosinophilic meningitis.  The vast majority of these cases are caused by rat lungworm. Daily flights between Honolulu and Manila were routine back in the 1970s and 1980s and may even have been profitable, but that was when the distance was about max for airplanes at that time and they had to stop here to refuel before continuing on to the US mainland.  The Honolulu to Manila market disintegrated when airlines began using long range airplanes that could fly non-stop between Manila and the US West Coast.  The same situation occurred with Singapore Airlines.   Travelers then had to be routed through Guam or Tokyo which added several hours in travel times.  Hawaiian canceled its direct service because it was not profitable. It remains to be seen if PAL can defy the odds.

Though they deserve our great respect, constitutions can never adequately address every circumstance. For those exceptional situations we have politics, and in Canada's case the notwithstanding clause.
